prompt: Programmatic poster brief: render the machine-learning concept gradient descent inside authentic Bauhaus graphic design grammar from 1919-1933. Use asymmetrical layout, black construction rules, geometric sans-serif typography, flat planes of primary color, circles and rectangles arranged on a strict grid, and an off-white paper ground. The concept should remain legible as a red state moving step by step down a diagonal sequence of planes into concentric circular minima. No photorealism, no 3D shading, no generic tech icons; make it read as a Bauhaus poster rather than a modern infographic.
tradition_approach: I used Bauhaus asymmetry, geometric sans type, black construction lines, off-white paper, and flat red-blue-yellow forms so the optimization path reads through the movement grammar of the poster.
